title: Fixture: Public Health Data Support
agency: Department of Health and Human Services
sourceText:
SAMPLE FIXTURE — FOR DEMONSTRATION ONLY

Solicitation No. 75P001-26-Q-0098. The Department of Health and Human Services seeks data engineering and program analytics support for a public-health reporting office. NAICS 541611 applies. This procurement is an 8(a) competitive set-aside. The Government anticipates a labor-hour task order with a one-year base period and two one-year options. Questions are due July 29, 2026, and quotations are due August 12, 2026.

The contractor shall maintain data pipelines, validate monthly submissions from state partners, produce executive dashboards, and prepare recurring data-quality reports. The contractor must provide a transition-in plan within ten business days of award and complete transition within thirty days. Monthly status reports shall describe accomplishments, risks, staffing, and corrective actions. Dashboard and report deliverables must meet applicable Section 508 requirements. No artificial intelligence or machine-learning development is included in the scope.

The contractor shall provide a program manager, senior data engineer, data-quality analyst, and visualization specialist. The program manager must hold a PMP certification. Key personnel substitutions require contracting-officer approval. Quotations must identify labor categories, loaded hourly rates, and estimated hours. Offerors must submit two examples performed within the last five years that demonstrate similar public-health data integration and reporting work.

Evaluation factors are technical approach, staffing, past performance, and price. Technical approach and staffing are equal in importance and, when combined with past performance, are more important than price. Quotations must be submitted through the agency portal as a single searchable PDF no later than the stated deadline. FAR 52.204-7, FAR 52.204-25, FAR 52.219-14, and applicable 8(a) clauses are incorporated.
